channel.title: U4Claude
channel.description: Updates to U4Claude (Arcanii Mod) — Claude.ai usage menu bar app for macOS.

item.title: v1.5.0
item.description:
v1.5.0 — API refactor + test coverage

No user-visible changes. Same UI, same data flow, same widget. Internal-only release.

  • Response models (UsageResponse, ExtraUsageResponse, UsageData, ExtraUsageData, etc.) extracted into a dependency-free file so the SwiftPM test target can cover them.
  • 24 new tests for toUsageData() and toExtraUsageData() — including the legacy-field fallback for Extra Usage and the fractional-second rounding for reset times. Test count 11 → 35.
  • fetchOrganizations migrated to async throws -> [Organization]; all three callsites (auth settings, welcome, web login) updated together.

See the full release notes.

item.title: v1.4.1
item.description:
v1.4.1 — Quick-wins polish

  • Ring illumination slider. New "Popover Appearance" card in General Settings — drag from 0% (off) through subtle to 100% (vivid, the v1.4.0 default) to dial the popover ring glow. Above 50% the macOS-26 Liquid Glass material is layered in; below it the slider just dims the surrounding shadows.
  • Auto-relogin recovers from a dismissed login window. The session-expired throttle now clears on explicit retry (manual refresh or popover-open fetch), so the WebLogin window re-prompts on the next failure instead of latching forever.
  • Spoofed Chrome user-agent bumped 140 → 148 to keep up with Cloudflare's heuristics.

See the full release notes.

item.title: v1.4.0
item.description:
v1.4.0 — Desktop widget

Drag the new "Claude Usage" widget onto your desktop (Edit Widgets → search for it). Two sizes:

  • Small — single ring with percentage and reset countdown.
  • Medium — both 5-hour and 7-day rings side-by-side.

The widget reads from an App Group snapshot the main app writes on every successful fetch — no extra API calls, no Cloudflare to dodge, no session-expiry handling. WidgetCenter is pinged on each fetch so the rings update immediately.

Also: switched the build script to automatic signing with -allowProvisioningUpdates so the App Group entitlements work end-to-end without pre-issued provisioning profiles.

item.title: v1.3.2
item.description:
v1.3.2 — Sparkle plumbing fix

v1.3.0 and v1.3.1 shipped without working Sparkle. The Sparkle keys were silently dropped from the auto-generated Info.plist, and the menu's "Check for updates…" handler had a flaky AppDelegate cast. This release fixes both:

  • Static Config/Info.plist with SUFeedURL, SUPublicEDKey, and SUEnableAutomaticChecks baked in.
  • AppDelegate.shared static reference replaces the unreliable NSApp.delegate as? AppDelegate cast.

v1.3.0/v1.3.1 users will need to install this manually — their Sparkle has no feed URL and can't auto-update. After v1.3.2, all future updates flow normally.

item.title: v1.3.1
item.description:
v1.3.1 — Glassy glow on the popover rings

Cosmetic release. The 5-hour and 7-day rings now render as glowing glass tubes:

  • 4-stop linear gradient on the stroke for a translucent glass-tube body.
  • Tight color-matched shadows for a lit-from-within glow without the broad fog.
  • On macOS 26+, the rings additionally pick up the system Liquid Glass material via .glassEffect(in:). Older macOS keeps the gradient-only fallback.

No data, API, or build-pipeline changes — pure UI.

item.title: v1.3.0
item.description:
v1.3.0 — Sparkle in-app updates

This is the first release where updates install with one click via Sparkle, replacing the old "download → drag-to-Applications → relaunch" flow.

  • Updates are EdDSA signature-verified before install — a tampered or corrupted DMG is rejected.
  • Background check still 24h cadence; manual check available from the right-click menu.
  • Custom UpdateChecker deleted (~290 lines); DataRefreshManager is now strictly about Claude usage data.

See the full release notes for the migration story and what's deferred.

item.title: v1.2.0
item.description:
v1.2.0 — Auth-failure UX overhaul, multi-account ergonomics, history export

  • Auto-prompt re-login when the session expires — no more "right-click → Auth Settings" detour.
  • ⌘1–⌘9 keyboard shortcuts for switching between configured accounts.
  • Rolling usage history capture + CSV export from the diagnostics view.
  • Better Cloudflare vs. session-invalid distinction in the API layer.
  • About tab simplified, log messages normalized to English, Chrome user-agent bumped to 140.

See the full release notes for the complete changelog and internal cleanup.
